title: Self-Reflection Security Prompting — 47-56% Vulnerability Reduction
description: Two-stage pattern: generate code, then review as security engineer. Single most effective mitigation for solo devs
steps:
Generate feature code normally
Prompt: act as Security Engineer, review for OWASP top 10
Add security persona to CLAUDE.md project rules
Ban dangerous patterns in rule files
Run SAST as pre-commit hook

title: Plan-and-Execute Model Routing — Cut Multi-Agent Costs 90%
description: Use frontier model for planning, cheap models for execution. 80-90% of agent work is execution that does not need expensive models
steps:
Designate planner agent with frontier model
Create executor agents with cheap models
Classify subtask complexity for routing
Use batch APIs for non-urgent tasks
Enable prompt caching on planner system prompt
Measure savings: 85% execution = 76.5% cost cut

title: Three-Tier Markdown Agent Memory — Zero Infrastructure
description: Use MEMORY.md + daily logs + SOUL.md for agent persistence. Costs /bin/zsh.02/GB/month, Git-versioned, human-readable
steps:
Create MEMORY.md at project root
Create memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md daily logs
Create SOUL.md for agent identity
Give agent read/write + grep access
Distill learnings at session end
Add vector search only when memory exceeds context window

title: SHIELD Framework — Six Governance Controls for Vibe Coding
description: Implement Separation, Human-in-loop, Input validation, Enforce helper models, Least agency, Defensive controls
steps:
Restrict agents to dev/test only
Mandate human code review before merge
Sanitize prompts via guardrails
Deploy security review agents as pre-merge gate
Grant minimum permissions
Run SCA on all third-party components

title: Google Workspace CLI — 50+ Google APIs via One MCP Server
description: Install gws CLI with built-in MCP server exposing Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Chat, and 40+ more APIs
steps:
npm install -g @googleworkspace/cli
Run gws auth setup
Start MCP server: gws mcp -s drive,gmail,calendar
Connect to Claude Code via .mcp.json
Test with gws drive files list
For CI: gws auth export --unmasked

title: GPT-5.4 Tool Search — Cut Agent Token Costs 47%
description: Use tool_search parameter to defer loading tool schemas until runtime, reducing tokens 47% with zero accuracy loss
steps:
Enable tool_search in Responses API
Define full tool catalog
Model loads only relevant schemas at runtime
Preserve phase metadata on outputs
Use allowed_tools for per-request safety

title: Automate Google Workspace with gws CLI and MCP Server
description: Turn Google Workspace into agent-accessible toolkit via CLI
steps:
Install npm -g @googleworkspace/cli
Authenticate with gws auth login
Start MCP server with gws mcp
Use -s flag to restrict exposed services
Connect to Claude Code via .mcp.json
Pin version for stability (pre-v1.0)

title: Harden MCP Servers Against Prompt Injection (30 CVEs/60 Days)
description: Production hardening for MCP servers with isolation and signing
steps:
Run every MCP server in isolated gVisor/Firecracker container
Implement strict input validation on all endpoints
Deploy MCPTox and MindGuard for real-time scanning
Use digitally signed version-locked tools
Apply least-privilege with dynamic policy enforcement
Maintain complete inventory with continuous audit logging
Add human-in-the-loop for critical operations

title: Defend Against CyberStrikeAI Behavioral Attack Patterns
description: Behavioral detection for AI-augmented attacks targeting edge devices
steps:
Prioritize behavioral detection over IOC matching
Harden perimeter device management interfaces
Implement credential hygiene at scale
Deploy network segmentation for 27-second breakout
Monitor for LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning
Scan for CyberStrikeAI banner on port 8080

title: Harden Vibe Coding with Security Rule Files (47-56% Vuln Reduction)
description: Add security personas and ban patterns to rule files
steps:
Add security-aware persona to rule file
Prepend CWE-preventing instructions to prompts
Ban eval, exec, hardcoded secrets, shell=True patterns
Use Wiz open-source security rule templates
Version control rule files with PR review
Use rulesync to keep rules synced across tools

title: Use GPT-5.4 1M Context with Compaction and Tool Search
description: Build long-horizon agent workflows with deferred tool loading
steps:
Structure project with AGENTS.md files at each directory
Enable compaction for sessions beyond context threshold
Implement tool_search for deferred tool loading
Preserve phase fields on assistant output items
Maximize parallelism with multi_tool_use.parallel

title: Set Up Claude Code Review with REVIEW.md
description: Configure multi-agent PR review with custom rules
steps:
Install Claude GitHub App with read/write permissions
Select repos and trigger mode (PR creation or every push)
Create REVIEW.md with Always check, Style, and Skip sections
Leverage CLAUDE.md files for directory-level rules
Monitor costs at claude.ai/analytics/code-review

title: Sandbox Agentic Workflows with Kernel-Level Isolation
description: Use VMs, Kata containers, or WebAssembly for agent sandboxing instead of shared-kernel containers
steps:
Assess current sandbox kernel sharing
Deploy Kata containers or Firecracker VMs
Use WebAssembly for lightweight agents
Block network egress except whitelisted MCP
Add OS-level seccomp/Seatbelt defense-in-depth

title: Defend Against MCP Sampling Prompt Injection Attacks
description: Layer defenses against Unit 42 demonstrated MCP server sampling attack vectors
steps:
Audit MCP servers for sampling capabilities
Implement request validation on client/host
Apply strict output isolation
Whitelist only verified MCP servers
Monitor for token consumption anomalies

title: Build Claude Code PreToolUse Security Hooks for Bash Gates
description: Write regex-based PreToolUse hooks to block destructive Bash commands with agent feedback
steps:
Create shell script with regex patterns for destructive commands
Exit code 2 to block with stderr reason
Register in .claude/settings.json hooks
Test blocked command handling
Add PostToolUse audit trail logging

title: Detect AI-Generated Vibeware Malware via Behavioral Indicators
description: Identify APT36 vibeware through behavioral fingerprints across Nim/Zig/Crystal variants
steps:
Configure EDR for unsigned binaries in user-writable dirs
Monitor network for Discord/Slack/Supabase C2
Enable cross-language behavioral monitoring
Scan for AI-generation artifacts in metadata
Group samples by behavior not hash

title: Write Natural-Language Agent Policies with AWS AgentCore Cedar
description: Use AgentCore Policy GA to enforce deterministic agent-tool access controls via natural language
steps:
Set up AgentCore Gateway with starter toolkit
Write policy in plain English
Review generated Cedar policy
Attach to Gateway and test
Layer multiple policies for defense-in-depth

title: Deploy Codex Security for Continuous Vulnerability Scanning
description: Use OpenAI Codex Security agent for agentic vulnerability scanning with project-specific threat models
steps:
Connect GitHub repo to Codex Cloud
Create scan with commit history window
Review findings with severity and validation evidence
Integrate with CI/CD for PR-merge scanning
Monitor precision improvement over time

title: Anti-Distillation Behavioral Fingerprinting
description: Detect API distillation attacks via behavioral classifiers: topic diversity, formatting consistency, conversational pivots.
steps:
Build session-level behavioral classifier
Detect chain-of-thought elicitation at scale
Implement cross-account coordination detection
Deploy response shaping for suspected accounts
Share indicators with other API providers

title: Claude Code Worktree Hooks — Per-Agent Policy Enforcement
description: v2.1.69 ships agent identity in every hook event. Apply different security policies to different agents in parallel worktrees.
steps:
Create PreToolUse hook reading agent.name from payload
Use worktree metadata for per-worktree logging
Set up per-agent tool restrictions via identity checks
Combine with TeammateIdle hooks for auto-reassignment

title: Context Engineering Four-Strategy Framework
description: LangChain formalized Write/Select/Compress/Isolate strategies for systematic context management replacing ad-hoc prompt stuffing.
steps:
Write: persist intermediate findings to external state
Select: add RAG retrieval at decision points
Compress: summarize tool outputs before entering conversation
Isolate: separate context types in state schema
Track with LangSmith for tokens-per-step monitoring

title: Rivet Sandbox Agent SDK — Universal API for Any Coding Agent
description: One HTTP/SSE API to control Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Amp in sandboxes. Universal event schema for all agents.
steps:
Install @rivet-dev/sandbox-agent or docker compose up
Start session via POST /sessions with agent and workspace
Stream events via GET /sessions/id/events
Handle approvals via POST /sessions/id/approve
Persist to Postgres or ClickHouse for replay and audit

title: Agent Safehouse — Kernel-Level macOS Sandbox in 60 Seconds
description: Zero-dependency macOS sandbox using Apple sandbox-exec Seatbelt. Deny-first posture blocks syscalls at kernel. 12+ agents supported.
steps:
curl -fsSL agent-safehouse.dev/install.sh | bash
Run agent inside sandbox: safehouse claude from project dir
Use Policy Builder for custom capabilities
Create desktop launchers for one-click sandboxed sessions

title: GPT-5.4 Tool Search — Cut Token Usage 47%
description: Replace sending all tool definitions upfront with lightweight tool index. Model dynamically loads only needed definitions at runtime.
steps:
Register tools in tool_search parameter via Responses API
Provide lightweight name+description index instead of full schemas
Monitor usage object for 40-50% token reduction
Combine with reasoning effort none for 60%+ total savings

title: Hybrid Transformer-RNN Architecture for 2x Training Efficiency
description: OLMo Hybrid's 3:1 DeltaNet-to-attention pattern achieves 2x data efficiency. Gives state tracking plus precise recall in one model.
steps:
Download allenai/Olmo-Hybrid-7B from HuggingFace
Understand 3:1 GDN-to-attention layer pattern
Apply DRoPE for long-context tasks
Fine-tune with half the normal token budget
Ablate ratio starting from 3:1 baseline

title: Rapid Prototyping with Google Canvas AI Mode
description: Use Google Canvas in AI Mode to go from description to working web app prototype in minutes. Powered by Gemini 3 with live preview.
steps:
Open Google Search AI Mode and select Canvas
Describe tool with specific constraints
Test in Canvas side panel sandboxed iframe
Toggle to code view for manual edits
Iterate conversationally for features and fixes

title: Build API Distillation Detection for Model Endpoints
description: Implement behavioral fingerprinting to detect systematic distillation attacks. Organic users make typos and change topics; bots follow systematic sequences.
steps:
Build classifier for systematic topic coverage and formatted prompts
Detect chain-of-thought elicitation patterns at scale
Implement cross-account coordination detection
Apply response shaping for suspected accounts
Tighten identity verification for abused pathways

title: Claude Code Agent Teams for Parallel Development
description: Spawn multiple Claude Code instances that share a task list and self-coordinate for parallel code review, debugging, and cross-layer development.
steps:
Enable CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS in settings.json
Request a team with distinct roles
Use Shift+Down to cycle between teammates
Size tasks at 5-6 per teammate
Enforce quality gates via TeammateIdle and TaskCompleted hooks

title: OS-Level Agent Sandbox with Tiered Access Control
description: Use kernel-level sandboxing (Seatbelt/Bubblewrap/AppContainer) for agentic workflows. Application-level controls fail when code passes to subprocesses.
steps:
Block all network egress by default for sandbox processes
Enforce file write restrictions at OS level
Use explicit secret injection instead of env inheritance
Never cache approval decisions across executions
Implement ephemeral sandbox lifecycle

title: Defend Against Vibeware DDoD with Behavioral Process Monitoring
description: Shift from signature-based to behavioral detection for AI-generated polyglot malware. Monitor for Living Off Trusted Services patterns and Unicode emoji artifacts.
steps:
Configure EDR for unsigned binaries from user-writable dirs
Create rules for outbound Slack/Discord/Supabase from unverified binaries
Monitor for Unicode emoji strings in binary code
Implement multi-runtime memory injection detection
Deploy dynamic attack surface reduction

title: Build Outcome-Based Pricing for AI-Native SaaS Products
description: Six proven pricing models for the seat-to-usage transition
steps:
Start with usage-based for early stage
Graduate to hybrid subscription + overage
Implement prepaid credits
Add outcome-based pricing as you mature
Use tiered volume pricing for enterprise
Use billing engine like Lago or Stripe

title: Build Event-Driven Multi-Agent Systems with Kafka
description: Four multi-agent patterns transformed into event-driven distributed systems using Kafka
steps:
Orchestrator-Worker via Kafka command topic
Hierarchical agent pattern with tier-specific topics
Blackboard pattern with shared state topic
Market-based pattern with auction topic
Implement event persistence for replay
Add Flink for stream processing

title: Hoard Working Examples for Agent-Amplified Recombination
description: Willison pattern: every working code example becomes a composable building block for agents
steps:
Start personal working-examples repo
Document the how not just what
Point agents at your examples
Use cross-project learning
Build single-page HTML tools
Compound over time

title: Implement OWASP Secure MCP Server Development Checklist
description: MCP-specific security checklist from OWASP covering tool poisoning, confused deputy, memory poisoning
steps:
Implement OAuth 2.1 with Token Delegation
Session isolation with deterministic cleanup
Validate all inputs AND outputs against JSON Schema
Container isolation as non-root
Enforce TLS 1.2+
Hash-verify tool descriptions

title: Apply Context Engineering to Cut Agent Token Costs 60-80%
description: Hierarchical budget allocation and dynamic tool filtering for production agents
steps:
Allocate token budgets by tier
Implement dynamic tool filtering (max 15)
Enable automatic compaction at 70%
Structure for prompt caching
Apply four-tier context hierarchy
Monitor quality metrics

title: Set Up Cursor Automations for Event-Driven Coding Agents
description: Cursor Automations lets you build always-on coding agents that fire on external events
steps:
Choose trigger (GitHub, Slack, PagerDuty, Linear, cron)
Write agent instructions
Configure MCPs and models
Set output actions
Test in sandbox
Enable memory

title: XML Tag Architecture for Claude Prompts
description: Claude models trained to recognize XML tags as structural delimiters. Use 4-block pattern: instructions, context, task, output_format. XML outperforms Markdown for creating unambiguous boundaries. Nest tags for multi-document context. Use constraints tag for hard boundaries.

title: RAGAS RAG Pipeline Quality Gates
description: Automate RAG quality gates in CI with RAGAS metrics: ContextPrecision, ContextRecall, Faithfulness, ResponseRelevancy. Create golden test sets of 50-100 questions, assert thresholds in CI. Use RAGAS synthetic test generation for coverage expansion.

title: CyberStrikeAI Infrastructure Hardening
description: Harden against AI-augmented attacks like CyberStrikeAI FortiGate campaign (600+ devices, 55 countries). Audit management port exposure, enforce MFA on all management interfaces, monitor for DCSync and lateral movement, implement network segmentation, patch perimeter devices within 48 hours of CVE.

title: OLMo Hybrid 7B Local Deployment
description: OLMo Hybrid 7B replaces 75% of attention layers with gated DeltaNet heads. 75% better inference throughput on long contexts. Deploy via vLLM with --enforce-eager --disable-cascade-attn flags. Apache 2.0 with fully open weights, data, training code.

title: PEV Loop for Reliable Agent Code
description: Plan-Execute-Verify loop replacing prompt-and-hope. Plan with precise specs and verification criteria. Execute with specialized agent roles. Verify with deterministic checks first, then agentic validation. Allow up to 3 self-correction iterations.

title: Anthropic Distillation Defense
description: Four-part defense against model distillation: behavioral fingerprinting, velocity limits, response shaping, intelligence sharing. DeepSeek used 24K fraudulent accounts and 16M exchanges to distill Claude.

title: Fault-Tolerant Agent Checkpoint Recovery
description: Four-layer fault tolerance: retry with backoff+jitter, model fallback chains, error classification, checkpoint recovery. Drops unrecoverable failures from 23% to under 2%.

title: Mermaid Context Compression
description: Mermaid diagrams in CLAUDE.md compress architecture context 10x vs prose. Claude reads Mermaid natively as structural maps. Encode architecture as flowcharts, sequence diagrams, ER diagrams under 50 lines each.

title: Elastic MCP Four-Layer Defense
description: Four-layer MCP defense: sandboxing, authorization boundaries, tool integrity verification via mcp-scan, runtime monitoring. Most MCP clients dont re-prompt on tool description changes enabling rug-pull attacks.

title: Agent Teams Worktree Orchestration
description: Claude Code Agent Teams with worktree isolation for parallel AI development. 3-5 parallel instances coordinate via peer-to-peer messaging, claim shared tasks. --worktree gives each agent its own branch. 2x faster, 2.5x tokens.

title: Custom Claude Code Subagents
description: Build scoped subagents in .claude/agents/ with isolated worktrees

title: Pentagon-Proof Vendor Diversification
description: LiteLLM abstraction plus eval parity across providers

title: OAuth 2.1 PKCE for Remote MCP Servers
description: Secure remote MCP servers with OAuth 2.1 and PKCE

title: Siri App Intents Schema Conformance
description: Expose apps to Gemini-powered Siri via App Intents

title: Oregon SB 1546 Chatbot Compliance
description: AI disclosure, crisis protocols, minor protections

title: Custom Claude Code Slash Commands
description: Ship team slash commands in .claude/commands/

title: Three-Layer API Distillation Defense
description: Behavioral classifiers and response shaping to prevent model extraction

title: CyberStrikeAI Countermeasures
description: Defend against AI-powered recon

title: Parallel Claude Code via Worktrees
description: Use --worktree flag for parallel agent sessions

title: Claude-Resilient Multi-Provider Failover
description: Build failover chain with LiteLLM proxy for Claude outage resilience

title: Hybrid Prompt Architecture
description: Combine system prompts, few-shot examples, and dynamic context retrieval for production agent systems.
steps:
1. Define static system prompt with role, constraints, output format
2. Add 2-3 few-shot examples covering common and edge cases
3. Implement dynamic context injection from RAG or knowledge base
4. Use structured output schemas for consistent parsing
5. A/B test prompt variants with LLM-as-Judge evaluation

title: Verify-Before-Submit Pattern (GLM-5 Style)
description: Add a verification step to agent workflows where the model checks its own output against requirements before submitting.
steps:
1. Add explicit verification prompt after generation step
2. Include original requirements in verification context
3. Use structured output for pass/fail with reasoning
4. On fail: loop back with specific feedback, max 3 retries
5. Log verification results for quality monitoring

title: n8n SaaS Replacement Pipeline
description: Replace paid SaaS tools with n8n workflows powered by AI agents for common business operations.
steps:
1. Identify top 3 SaaS tools by monthly spend
2. Map their core workflows to n8n node equivalents
3. Add AI agent nodes for classification, summarization, routing
4. Connect to existing APIs (Slack, email, CRM) via n8n integrations
5. Run parallel with SaaS tool for 2 weeks, then cut over

title: MCP Supply Chain Defense
description: Protect your agent skill pipeline from malicious packages using SkillFortify-style verification and runtime monitoring.
steps:
1. Audit all installed MCP servers and skills for known CVEs
2. Pin specific versions in your MCP config -- no floating tags
3. Run SkillFortify or similar scanner on dependency graphs
4. Enable runtime monitoring for unexpected network calls from tools
5. Implement allowlist for tool capabilities per MCP server

title: Voice-First Development: 3x Speed with Claude Code /voice
description: Use Claude Code voice mode for architectural discussions and refactoring while maintaining visual code review.
steps:
1. Enable with /voice command in Claude Code terminal
2. Use push-to-talk (hold spacebar) for commands
3. Describe architectural changes verbally while reviewing code visually
4. Use voice for refactoring instructions, typing for precise edits
5. Combine with ultrathink for complex reasoning tasks

title: LLM-as-Judge with DeepEval in CI/CD
description: Set up automated LLM evaluation in your CI pipeline using DeepEval to catch quality regressions before deployment.
steps:
1. pip install deepeval and configure API keys
2. Define evaluation metrics: faithfulness, relevance, toxicity
3. Create test cases from production query logs
4. Add deepeval test command to CI pipeline
5. Set quality gates: fail build if score drops below threshold

title: MCP Server Audit: Top 5 Vulnerability Patterns
description: Systematic audit checklist for MCP server security covering the most common vulnerability patterns found in 30+ recent CVEs.
steps:
1. Check for command injection in tool parameter handling
2. Verify authentication on all tool endpoints (no default open)
3. Test for SSRF via URL parameters in fetch/request tools
4. Audit file path handling for directory traversal
5. Review token/credential storage -- no plaintext, no logs

title: Cursor .mdc Token Tax: 60% Context Reduction
description: Optimize Cursor rule files to reduce context window consumption by 60% using conditional loading and progressive disclosure.
steps:
1. Audit current .mdc files -- measure token count per file
2. Split monolithic rules into domain-specific files (auth, db, ui)
3. Add globs field to each .mdc for conditional loading
4. Move examples to @-referenced files loaded only when needed
5. Measure before/after token usage on typical coding sessions

title: Agent Identity Principals Pattern
description: Implement identity management for AI agents using human sponsorship, dynamic access tokens, and comprehensive audit trails.
steps:
1. Assign each agent a human sponsor who owns its access scope
2. Replace long-lived API keys with short-lived dynamic tokens
3. Implement per-action audit logging with agent ID and sponsor
4. Set up privilege decay -- agents lose unused permissions after N days
5. Deploy guardian agent to monitor for privilege escalation

title: Claude Code Hooks as Security Guard
description: Use Claude Code hooks to auto-scan tool outputs for secrets, validate file changes against allowlists, and block dangerous commands before execution.
steps:
1. Create .claude/hooks.json with pre-tool and post-tool hooks
2. Add secret-scanning regex patterns for API keys, tokens, credentials
3. Wire post-edit hook to run git-secrets or trufflehog on changed files
4. Add pre-bash hook to block rm -rf, DROP TABLE, and force-push patterns
5. Test with intentional bad patterns to verify blocking

title: Apple App Intents 2.0 for Siri Agent Integration
description: iOS 26 dev beta live. Action inference from UI reduces integration burden. Core ML 4.0 on-device 45 TOPS.
steps:
Adopt App Intents framework with @AssistantIntent
Define entities with @AssistantEntity
Leverage action inference from SwiftUI views
Test with Siri on-screen awareness
Use Core ML 4.0 for on-device AI

title: Claude Code Agent Teams Mailbox Pattern
description: 2-16 agents with peer-to-peer messaging via Mailbox. Shared Task List coordination. 7x tokens but parallelizes hours to minutes.
steps:
Enable agent teams experimental flag
Write clear CLAUDE.md before spawning
Use TeamCreate then Task with team_name
Use SendMessage for cross-agent coordination
Best for parallel reviews and cross-layer changes

title: mcp-scan CI Pipeline with Tool Pinning
description: Hash-based MCP tool manifest pinning detects rug pulls. 3-line GitHub Actions YAML integration. npm audit for MCP servers.
steps:
Install mcp-scan via pip or npx
Run initial baseline scan to hash manifests
Add to CI with invariantlabs/mcp-scan-action
Enable rug-pull detection on subsequent scans
Complement with Snyk agent-scan

title: OPA-Based Least-Privilege AI Agent Gateway
description: Policy-as-code with Rego enforces per-action permissions at API boundary for agent tool calls. Ephemeral runners limit blast radius.
steps:
Deploy API gateway between agent and services
Write Rego policies per agent role
Query OPA for every tool-call
Use ephemeral runners with <5min TTL
Add OpenTelemetry traces for audit

title: Claude Code Auto-Memory Plus Subagent Memory
description: Subagents accumulate their own memory across sessions and share across git worktrees. Compounds over 5-10 sessions.
steps:
Verify auto-memory toggle via /memory
Check captures in ~/.claude/projects/memory/
Leverage subagent memory for specialized agents
Share memory across worktrees

title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking-Level Routing
description: Use minimal/low/medium/high thinking levels to cut costs 4x on simple tasks while activating Deep Think Mini for complex reasoning
steps:
Classify API calls by complexity tier
Set thinking_level in generation config
Use LOW/MINIMAL for long-output tasks
Analyze thought summaries to calibrate levels

title: Master Plan-Iterate-Test Loop for Production Vibe Coding
description: Structured vibe coding: CLAUDE.md conventions, one feature at a time, immediate testing, version control as undo
steps:
Start with CLAUDE.md defining stack and conventions
Describe one feature at a time
Test immediately after each generation
Iterate with specific file and function references
Commit after each working feature
Graduate to spec-driven development

title: Implement Dynamic RAG Retrieval with Query-Adaptive Strategies
description: Match retrieval complexity to query complexity: lightweight for factual, multi-stage for analytical, temporal filtering for recency
steps:
Classify query as factual, analytical, comparative, or temporal
Factual: single-pass vector search k=3
Analytical: broad search k=20 + cross-encoder reranking
Temporal: add timestamp filtering
Use hybrid BM25 + vector with RRF
Continuous eval with Ragas or DeepEval

title: Detect Distillation Attacks with Behavioral Fingerprinting
description: Build behavioral classifiers, metadata fingerprinting, digital watermarking, and semantic rate limits to detect capability extraction
steps:
Train classifiers on extraction vs normal usage patterns
Track IP correlation and request timing
Embed invisible watermarks in responses
Set semantic diversity rate limits
Share threat intelligence cross-provider

title: Optimize RAG Chunking with 512-Token Recursive Strategy
description: Use recursive character splitting at 512 tokens as optimal default. Skip overlap. Only use semantic chunking for structured docs.
steps:
Start with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter at 400-512 tokens
Match chunk size to query type
Skip chunk overlap
Only semantic chunking for clear logical boundaries
Implement semantic caching at 0.90-0.95 threshold

title: Defend Against MCP Tool Poisoning with Defense-in-Depth
description: Implement schema validation, least privilege, container isolation, human-in-the-loop gates, and audit logging for MCP
steps:
Validate JSON-RPC against strict schemas
Configure minimum necessary permissions per tool
Run third-party MCP servers in Docker containers
Disable auto-approval for sensitive tools
Log all tool interactions
Audit for shadow MCP servers

title: Three-Tier Context Layering with Claude Code Skills
description: Use progressive disclosure across global CLAUDE.md, project CLAUDE.md, and skills files to cut token costs 90%+
steps:
Create .claude/skills/ directory
Write metadata with globs and description triggers
Keep skill body under 5000 tokens
Layer context: global > project > task-specific
Combine with subagents for portable expertise

title: Apply Structured Note-Taking for Long-Horizon Agent Tasks
description: Use Anthropic's structured note-taking pattern with NOTES.md and compaction triggers for long-running agents
steps:
Create NOTES.md at task start
Structure with Completed, In Progress, Blocked, Key Decisions
Implement compaction at 80% context
Persist to external store
Track dependencies explicitly

title: Build Claude Code Agent Teams with Peer-to-Peer Communication
description: Use Agent Teams for 2-16 independent Claude instances with peer-to-peer mailbox messaging on shared codebases
steps:
Main session becomes Team Lead using Task tool with team_name
Define clear role boundaries per teammate
Use SendMessage for peer-to-peer coordination
Coordinate via shared task list
Budget 7x tokens of single session

steps:
Install Spec Kit CLI
Specify phase: describe what and why
Plan phase: agent creates implementation plan
Tasks phase: break into actionable items
Build phase: agent implements against spec constraints
Commit specs alongside code

title: Secure MCP Servers with mcp-scan Tool Pinning
description: Use mcp-scan to detect tool poisoning, rug pull attacks, and prompt injection in MCP servers
steps:
Run uvx mcp-scan@latest to auto-discover MCP configs
Run inspect to view tool descriptions
Tool pinning hashes descriptions for change detection
Whitelist trusted tools
Add to CI/CD pipeline

title: RAG Chunking 512-Token Recursive Strategy
description: Recursive character splitting at 512 tokens outperforms complex AI-driven chunking in most cases. Skip overlap.
steps:
Start RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter at 400-512 tokens
Match chunk to query type: factoid 256-512, analytical 1024+
Skip chunk overlap
Move to semantic chunking only if clear boundaries AND baseline underperforms
Add semantic caching with 0.90-0.95 threshold

title: MCP Defense-in-Depth Against Tool Poisoning
description: Layer schema validation, least privilege, container isolation, human-in-the-loop gates, and audit logging for MCP servers
steps:

Least privilege per MCP tool
Run third-party MCP in Docker containers
Disable auto-approve for credential/network tools
Audit log all tool interactions
Monitor for shadow MCPs

title: Structured Note-Taking for Long-Horizon Agent Tasks

steps:

Structure with Completed, In Progress, Blocked, Key Decisions
Trigger compaction at 80% context fill
Persist notes to external store
Track dependencies explicitly

steps:

title: Spec-Driven Development with GitHub Spec Kit
description: Turn vague prompts into executable specs with acceptance criteria using GitHub Spec Kit 72.7k stars
steps:

Specify phase: describe what and why
Plan phase: agent creates implementation plan
Tasks phase: break into actionable items
Build phase: agent implements against spec

title: Secure MCP Servers with mcp-scan Tool Pinning

steps:
Run uvx mcp-scan@latest to auto-discover configs
Run inspect to view tool descriptions
First scan hashes descriptions for tool pinning
Whitelist trusted tools

title: Replace SaaS Point Solutions with AI Agent Workflows
description: Audit SaaS stack and replace commoditized point solutions with agent-driven automation
steps:
Audit subscriptions: commoditized vs differentiated
Estimate core workflow for each
Build replacement agents with Claude Code + MCP
Use outcome-based pricing for tools you build
Keep platforms with deep data moats
Start with internal tools first

title: Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in Agents
description: Multi-layer defense: sanitization, delimiter isolation, dual-LLM filtering, confirmation gates
steps:
Sanitize external data before agent context
Use delimiter-based isolation for tool outputs
Apply dual-LLM pattern for pre-filtering
Enforce confirmation gates for high-impact actions
Monitor for behavioral anomalies
Red-team with garak framework

title: Subagent Delegation with Context Isolation
description: Run subagents in isolated context windows with scoped tools and compact output summaries
steps:
Define subagents with clear description fields
Scope tools per subagent
Pass file paths not file contents
Write shared CLAUDE.md for all subagents
Design compact output summaries
Chain subagents with summary-only handoffs

title: Build Three-Tier CLAUDE.md Hierarchy for Teams
description: Use global, project, and subdirectory CLAUDE.md scopes for consistent agent output
steps:
Create ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md for personal defaults
Create project-root CLAUDE.md with build/test/lint commands
Add subdirectory CLAUDE.md for specialized contexts
Apply deletion test for each line
Use /init to generate starter
Review and prune monthly

title: Optimize RAG Chunking with Semantic Boundaries
description: Replace fixed-size chunking with semantic boundary detection for 60%+ faithfulness improvement
steps:
Replace fixed-size with semantic chunking
For code: chunk at function/class boundaries via AST
For prose: use sentence embedding similarity
Apply hybrid overlap for unstructured content
Implement smart routing to reduce retrieval 30-45%
Benchmark with RAGAS framework

title: Implement Least-Privilege MCP Server Sandboxing
description: Audit and sandbox MCP servers with OS-level isolation and credential management
steps:
Audit every MCP server permissions
Apply least-privilege per tool
Wrap in container sandbox
Implement auth on every connection
Store credentials in secrets manager
Bind to localhost only
Run agent-scan for misconfigurations

title: Structure Cursor Rules with .mdc Files
description: Replace monolithic .cursorrules with glob-scoped .mdc rule files for 96-98% compliance
steps:
Create .cursor/rules/ directory
Write focused .mdc files with gitignore-style patterns
Use concrete testable directives
Add rules for common AI mistakes
Reference multiple files in Composer
Migrate from legacy .cursorrules incrementally

title: One-Session-Per-Task Pattern with Handoff Summaries
description: Scope each Claude Code session to single task with explicit handoff to prevent context degradation
steps:
Scope each session to one task
Generate handoff summary before ending
Use /clear not /compact for fresh start
Paste handoff into new session
Keep persistent context in CLAUDE.md
Store plans in ~/.claude/plans/

title: Scan MCP Servers for Tool Poisoning with MindGuard
description: Use Decision Dependence Graphs to detect malicious tool descriptions with 95.3% accuracy
steps:
Install MindGuard as intermediary layer
Run MCPTox benchmark scan
Enable provenance tracking
Set detection rules for rug pull attacks
Integrate with SIEM
Schedule regular re-scans

title: Deploy Claude Code Agent Teams for Parallel Development
description: Orchestrate 2-16 parallel Claude sessions via shared task list for 3-4x speedup
steps:
Enable CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1
Use plan mode to decompose into parallel-safe subtasks
Spin up teammates with assigned slices
Each teammate reads shared CLAUDE.md
Lead synthesizes results and handles conflicts

title: System Prompt Hardening Against AI-Augmented Attacks
description: Defensive instructions making LLM apps resistant to instruction extraction and tool misuse
steps:
Add explicit defensive instructions
Implement tool-call verification
Separate system context with delimiters
Add canary tokens
Test against OWASP benchmarks
Layer with runtime guardrails

title: Shadow AI Agent Discovery and Containment
description: Find and contain unsanctioned AI agents before they become breach vectors
steps:
Scan for MCP traffic patterns
Audit cloud API key creation
Review CI/CD for agent dependencies
Implement SSO for MCP access
Deploy MCP gateway with RBAC
Establish agent registration policy
